approved_summary: Remediation v2 (Option A, user-approved 2026-07-16): validator converted to Tera Medium primary with paired M3 validator. Stage 7 selection is a PERSISTED STRICT-ALTERNATION counter (.conductor/validator-alternation.json), NOT SHA-256 parity. This guarantees exactly one Tera and one M3 validation per two consecutive runs. Plan reviewer retained M3 (Stage 1 creator is OpenAI SOL). Test runner on Tera Medium.
deterministic_alternation_rule: Read last_used from <workspace-root>\.conductor\validator-alternation.json; dispatch the opposite validator identity; flip last_used to the agent just used. Guarantees EXACT 50/50 Tera/M3 across consecutive validation runs. Replaces the rejected SHA-256 parity design.

assignments.invocation_rule: Stage 2 plan review always uses this agent identity
assignments.reason: Cannot change: creator (Stage 1) is OpenAI SOL; changing reviewer to Tera would create same-family pair

assignments.invocation_rule: Stage 6 test runner always uses this agent identity
assignments.reason: Changed to Tera Medium: executor (GLM) != test-runner (OpenAI Tera) preserves diversity

assignments.invocation_rule: Stage 7 validation uses this Tera primary when validator-alternation.json last_used=m3 (strict alternation)
assignments.reason: Changed to Tera Medium primary validator; paired with conductor-track-validator-m3 and selected by persisted strict-alternation counter for exact 50/50

assignments.invocation_rule: Stage 7 validation uses this M3 paired validator when validator-alternation.json last_used=tera (strict alternation)
assignments.reason: Paired M3 validator selected by persisted strict-alternation counter; exact 50/50 with Tera primary

indivisible_count_exception: Plan reviewer (Stage 2) ineligible for Tera: creator (Stage 1) is OpenAI SOL (same OpenAI family). With plan-reviewer fixed at M3, test-runner fixed at Tera, and validator strictly alternating Tera/M3, the checkpoint distribution over every two consecutive runs is exactly 3 M3 + 3 Tera = 50% each (hard guarantee, not statistical).
